'''Moses Mbye''' (born 13 August 1993) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[Ipswich Jets]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

Over the course of his career he has played as a {{rlp|sh|fb|ce|so}} and {{rlp|hk}}. He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), and at representative level for the [[Prime Minister's XIII]], [[World All Stars]] and [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] in the [[State of Origin series]].

==Background==
Mbye was born in [[Brisbane|Brisbane, Queensland, Australia]], and is of [[The Gambia|Gambian]] descent through his father, making him one of the few players in the NRL to be of African descent.<ref>{{Cite news |title=Moses Mbye is Looking to Follow Johnathan Thurston |work=[[Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|Daily Telegraph]] |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/african-moses-mbye-is-looking-to-follow-johnathan-thurston/story-e6freye9-1226624754343 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20140426093200/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/african-moses-mbye-is-looking-to-follow-johnathan-thurston/story-e6freye9-1226624754343 |url-status=dead |archive-date=26 April 2014 |access-date=2015-09-29}}</ref>

==Playing career==
[[File:mosesmbye.JPG|thumb|right|Mbye playing for the Bulldogs in 2014]]
Mbye played his junior rugby league for the [[Noosa Pirates]]. He played for the Pirates first grade side at age 17.<ref>{{Cite news |last1=Adams  |first1=Tony |last2=Gould |first2=Joel  |date=2015-08-10 |title=Mo$e$ in the Money! |work=[[Rugby League Week]]}}</ref>

===Early career===
From 2011 to 2013, Mbye played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{Cite web |title=M |url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/ |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150627005458/http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/ |archive-date=2015-06-27 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=18th Man|date=27 December 2012 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Moses Mbye |url=http://rleague.com/players/Moses-Mbye_3467/Stats/ |url-status=dead 2012, he played for the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team against the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-04-16 |title=Toyota U/20's Origin squads named |url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-u/20s-origin-squads-named/tabid/10874/newsid/66909/default.aspx |url-status=live |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=2012-04-19 |title=NSW v QLD under-20s Origin |work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-preview/nsw-v-qld-under20s-origin-20120419-1x9ki.html |url-status=live |url-access=limited |access-date=2014-11-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141011022630/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-preview/nsw-v-qld-under20s-origin-20120419-1x9ki.html |archive-date=2014-10-11}}</ref> In 2013, he captained the Bulldogs' NYC team.<ref>{{Cite tweet |number=324327772573036545 |user=NRL_Bulldogs |title=NYC Captain Moses Mbye joins the boys to tackle rep selection in this weeks episode of Zap's Wrap. |author=[[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] |date=2013-04-16 |access-date=2015-09-29}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-06-04 |title=Bankstown Public School Celebrates Reconciliation |url=http://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2013/06/04/bankstown-public-school-celebrates-reconciliation.html |url-status=live |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=[[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]}}</ref> On 20 April 2013, he again played for the Queensland under-20s team against the New South Wales under-20s team.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-04-15 |title=Under 20's Origin squads named |url=http://www.nrl.com/under-20s-origin-squads-named/tabid/10874/newsid/71757/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150106075314/http://www.nrl.com/under-20s-origin-squads-named/tabid/10874/newsid/71757/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-01-06 |access-date=2015-03-08 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-04-15 |title=Queensland Under 20s Team for Origin Match |website=[[Queensland Rugby League]]}}</ref> On 13 October 2013, he played for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-10-07 |title=Junior Kangaroos team named |url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-team-named/tabid/10874/newsid/75007/default.aspx |url-status=live |access-date=2014-11-23 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref>

===2014===
In 2014, Mbye moved on to Canterbury's [[New South Wales Cup]] team.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-03-04 |title=VB NSW Cup Team Lists - Round One |url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/03/04/vb_nsw_cup_team_lists_round_one.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150629232602/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/03/04/vb_nsw_cup_team_lists_round_one.html |archive-date=2015-06-29 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=[[New South Wales Rugby League]]}}</ref> He played for Canterbury in the inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] tournament.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-02-14 |title=Squads For Auckland Nines |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/ |archive-date=2016-01-15 |access-date=2015-06-27 |website=[[Rugby League Week]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-02-11 |title=Dogs name a mix of experience for Nines |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/dogs-mix-experience-nines/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141018215430/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/dogs-mix-experience-nines/ |archive-date=2014-10-18 |access-date=2014-11-23 |website=[[Rugby League Week]]}}</ref> On 14 March, he re-signed with the Canterbury outfit on a two-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Bulldogs Have Signed Moses Mbye for a Further Two Years |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-bulldogs-have-signed-queensland-young-gun-moses-mbye-for-a-further-two-years/story-e6frf3pu-1226854112552?from=public_rss |archive-url=https://archive.today/20140426135635/http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-bulldogs-have-signed-queensland-young-gun-moses-mbye-for-a-further-two-years/story-e6frf3pu-1226854112552?from=public_rss |url-status=dead |archive-date=26 April 2014 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-03-14 |title=Bulldogs re-sign young gun Moses Mbye |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/bulldogs-re-sign-young-gun-moses-mbye-12596/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150929110908/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/bulldogs-re-sign-young-gun-moses-mbye-12596/ |archive-date=2015-09-29 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=Zero Tackle}}</ref> In round 8, he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Canterbury against the [[Newcastle Knights]], playing at centre replacing an injured [[Tim Lafai]] in the 16-12 win at [[Stadium Australia|ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs 16 def. Newcastle Knights 12 |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/round-8/canterbury-vs-newcastle/summary.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150929171510/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/round-8/canterbury-vs-newcastle/summary.html |archive-date=2015-09-29 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-04-26 |title=NRL LATE MAIL: Bulldogs v Knights |url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2014/04/26/nrl_late_mail_bulldogs_v_knights.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141007045240/https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2014/04/26/nrl_late_mail_bulldogs_v_knights.html |archive-date=2014-10-07 |access-date=2014-11-23 |website=[[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]}}</ref> During the week leading up to the clubs [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Mbye and [[Reni Maitua]] were both in contention to fill the vacant hooker role for the match after regular hooker and captain [[Michael Ennis]] was ruled out with a foot injury.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Carayannis |first=Michael |date=2014-10-04 |title=Canterbury Bulldogs captain Michael Ennis ruled out of NRL grand final, James Graham, Trent Hodkinson named as co-captains |work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |agency=[[Australian Associated Press]] |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/canterbury-bulldogs-captain-michael-ennis-ruled-out-of-nrl-grand-final-james-graham-trent-hodkinson-named-as-cocaptains-20141004-10q8sc.html |access-date=2014-11-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141106021025/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/canterbury-bulldogs-captain-michael-ennis-ruled-out-of-nrl-grand-final-james-graham-trent-hodkinson-named-as-cocaptains-20141004-10q8sc.html |archive-date=2014-11-06}}</ref> Mbye was eventually given the nod over the more experienced Maitua in the 30-6 loss.<ref>{{Cite web |title=NRL 2014 - Grand Final |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/grand-final/south-sydney-vs-canterbury/summary.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150929181044/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2014/grand-final/south-sydney-vs-canterbury/summary.html |archive-date=2015-09-29 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=Rugby League Project}}</ref> He made 44 tackles, the most in the match.<ref>{{Cite news |title=Rabbitohs Win 2014 NRL Grand Final Defeating Caterbury Bulldogs 30-6 |work=[[Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|Daily Telegraph]] |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-win-2014-nrl-grand-final-defeating-canterbury-bulldogs-306/story-fni3fbgz-1227080755667 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20141005233031/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-win-2014-nrl-grand-final-defeating-canterbury-bulldogs-306/story-fni3fbgz-1227080755667 |url-status=dead |archive-date=5 October 2014 |access-date=2015-09-29}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-10-05 |title=Bulldogs Grand Final Player Ratings |url=http://www.nrl.com/bulldogs-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/82377/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141228051058/http://www.nrl.com/bulldogs-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/82377/default.aspx |archive-date=2014-12-28 |access-date=2014-11-23 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref>

===2015===
In the preseason, Mbye again played for the Canterbury club in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-01-23 |title=Bulldogs name 2015 Nines squad |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/bulldogs-2015-nines-squad/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304023627/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/bulldogs-2015-nines-squad/ |archive-date=2016-03-04 |access-date=2016-01-15 |website=Rugby League Week}}</ref> In 2015, he established himself as a regular part of the Bulldogs' side.<ref name="match-list">{{Cite web |title=Custom Match List |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NC0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDgwMy0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0= |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204211221/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NC0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDgwMy0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0= |archive-date=2016-02-04 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=Rugby League Project}}</ref> His form saw the interest for his signature in 2017 rise significantly.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-08-12 |title=Hot property Mbye to command big bucks |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/hot-property-mbye-to-command-big-bucks-18575/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150929103659/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/hot-property-mbye-to-command-big-bucks-18575/ |archive-date=2015-09-29 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=Zero Tackle}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-08-21 |title=Dogs fall short with Mbye offer |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/dogs-fall-short-with-mbye-offer-18694/#w4MYs0ICUfljCIHv.97 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150929131809/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/dogs-fall-short-with-mbye-offer-18694/ |archive-date=2015-09-29 |access-date=2015-09-29 |website=Zero Tackle}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Ryan |first=Nathan |date=2015-08-12 |title=Moses Mbye's next contract: With four clubs vying for his signature, where's the best fit for young gun? |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/moses-mbyes-next-contract-with-four-clubs-vying-for-his-signature-wheres-the-best-fit-for-young-gun/story-e6frf3pu-1227480508887 |access-date=2016-01-15 |website=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]}}</ref> In Round 4 against the [[Wests Tigers]], he kicked a field goal in golden-point extra time to win the match for Canterbury, 25-24.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-03-27 |title=Bulldogs beat Wests Tigers in golden point |url=http://www.nrl.com/bulldogs-beat-wests-tigers-in-golden-point/tabid/10874/newsid/84892/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151126120439/http://www.nrl.com/bulldogs-beat-wests-tigers-in-golden-point/tabid/10874/newsid/84892/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-11-26 |access-date=2016-01-15 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref> In Round 7 against the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], he scored his first NRL career try in the 28-16 win at ANZ Stadium.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs 28 def. Manly Warringah Sea Eagles 16 |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2015/round-7/canterbury-vs-manly/summary.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210912003837/https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2015/round-7/canterbury-vs-manly/summary.html |archive-date=2021-09-12 |access-date=2016-01-15 |website=Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Chammas |first=Michael |date=2015-04-17 |title=Manly Sea Eagles slump to fifth straight loss in 28-16 loss to Canterbury Bulldogs |work=[[Sydney Morning Herald]] |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/manly-sea-eagles-slump-to-fifth-straight-loss-in-2816-loss-to-canterbury-bulldogs-20150417-1mnoyg.html |url-status=live |access-date=2016-01-15 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160208014522/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/manly-sea-eagles-slump-to-fifth-straight-loss-in-2816-loss-to-canterbury-bulldogs-20150417-1mnoyg.html |archive-date=2016-02-08}}</ref> He finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in 23 matches, scoring three tries, kicking 15 goals and two field goals.<ref name="match-list" />

===2016===
On 12 January, Mbye was selected in the [[Queensland Academy of Sport]] Emerging Maroons squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Webeck |first=Tony |date=2016-01-12 |title=Maroons name Emerging Origin squad |url=http://www.nrl.com/maroons-name-emerging-origin-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/91313/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160114110023/http://www.nrl.com/maroons-name-emerging-origin-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/91313/default.aspx |archive-date=2016-01-14 |access-date=2016-01-15 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref> On 13 February, Mbye extended his contract with the Canterbury side to the end of the 2020 season, earning around $750,000 a season.<ref>{{Cite news |title=They are going to pay him how much?!? |work=[[The Courier-Mail]] |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/moses-mbye-to-stay-at-bulldogs-signing-3-million-fouryear-deal/news-story/38cd91eef978b9e5a9716faa56de5f1d |url-status=live |url-access=subscription |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170419190257/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/moses-mbye-to-stay-at-bulldogs-signing-3-million-fouryear-deal/news-story/38cd91eef978b9e5a9716faa56de5f1d |archive-date=2017-04-19}}</ref> Mbye finished the [[2016 NRL season]] playing in all of Canterbury's 25 matches and was their highest point scorer with 100 points by scoring nine tries and kicking 32 goals. On 24 September, Mbye played for [[Prime Minister's XIII]] against [[Papua New Guinea rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], playing off the interchange bench in the 58-0 win at [[Port Moresby]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Nicolussi |first=Christian |date=2016-09-24 |title=Prime Minister's XIII smashes Papua New Guinea 58-0 in Port Moresby |work=[[Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|Daily Telegraph]] |location=[[Port Moresby]] |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/prime-ministers-xiii-smashes-papua-new-guinea-580-in-port-moresby/news-story/4a60a5a5a71753d0107dc726e0e952c1 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180901113353/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/moses-mbye-to-stay-at-bulldogs-signing-3-million-fouryear-deal/news-story/38cd91eef978b9e5a9716faa56de5f1d |archive-date=2018-09-01}}</ref>

===2017===
On 10 February, Mbye played in the [[2017 All Stars match]] for the [[World All Stars]], starting at halfback in the 34-8 loss.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Crawley |first=Paul |date=2017-02-11 |title=Indigenous All Stars thump World All Stars 34-8 in Newcastle |work=[[Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|Daily Telegraph]] |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-thump-world-all-stars-348-in-newcastle/news-story/a016f102b1c7ddc5bbdeeedb83a149c5 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200711115736/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-thump-world-all-stars-348-in-newcastle/news-story/a016f102b1c7ddc5bbdeeedb83a149c5 |archive-date=2020-07-11}}</ref> In Round 18, at [[Belmore Sports Ground]], against the Newcastle Knights, the Bulldogs were losing 18-14 with two minutes remaining. A kick from Brock Lamb ricocheted into Mbye's legs and he then scooted away 80m to score. at the End of the year Moses Mbye was offered a contract from Samoa but denied it cause he wanted no injuries during the preseason.

Mbye finished his 2017 season with 22 games, scoring two tries and kicking 15 goals, with a total of 38 points.<ref>{{Cite news |title=Fairytale farewell for Belmore favourite |work=[[Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|Daily Telegraph]] |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/josh-reynolds-gets-winning-belmore-farewell-after-moses-mbye-brilliance/news-story/8f62437e0725c09280d3ab2407acde64 |url-access=subscription |access-date=2017-12-21}}</ref>

===2018===
On 29 May, Mbye signed a four-year deal to join the [[Wests Tigers]] starting 2019.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2018-05-29 |title=Moses Mbye jumps ship to Wests Tigers |language=en |work=[[news.com.au]] |agency=[[Australian Associated Press]] |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/moses-mbye-jumps-ship-to-wests-tigers/news-story/5a5a50584e6592bea785236ebce03004 |url-status=live |access-date=2018-06-04 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180615192101/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/moses-mbye-jumps-ship-to-wests-tigers/news-story/5a5a50584e6592bea785236ebce03004 |archive-date=2018-06-15}}</ref>  In June, he was granted a mid season switch to join Wests, he played his final game for Canterbury, a 32-10 defeat by the [[Gold Coast Titans]] at Belmore Oval which meant it was Canterbury's worst start to a season in 54 years.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2018-06-16 |title=Goodbye to Mbye as Bulldogs slump to worst start in 54 years |work=[[The Guardian]] |agency=[[Australian Associated Press]] |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/jun/16/goodbye-to-mbye-as-bulldogs-slump-to-worst-start-in-54-years |access-date=2018-06-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180618152313/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/jun/16/goodbye-to-mbye-as-bulldogs-slump-to-worst-start-in-54-years |archive-date=2018-06-18}}</ref>

Mbye made his first appearance for Wests Tigers on 1 July. He played in every game at fullback for the rest of the season, with the team winning 5 of those 9 matches, and scored 3 tries.{{citation needed|date=May 2024}}

===2019===
With the arrival of new coach [[Michael Maguire (rugby league)|Michael Maguire]], Mbye was named Wests Tigers' captain in the pre-season. Mbye contacted [[Benji Marshall]] and [[Jake Friend]], uncertain whether he should accept the position. Team-mate Marshall said, "I don’t think he understands that when he talks everyone listens. The thing I like the most is he doesn’t enjoy shit standards. He doesn’t walk past things that he doesn’t like. He drives standards really hard. I think that is a big reason why he is captain."<ref>{{Cite news |last=Read |first=Brent |title=Benji Marshall backs Moses Mbye to thrive as Wests Tigers captain |work=[[The Australian]] |url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/benji-marshall-backs-moses-mbye-to-thrive-as-wests-tigers-captain/news-story/cb888a5f6876f11dc54cb50128993059 |url-access=subscription |access-date=2019-03-14}}</ref>
Despite his club issues, Mbye played a starring role in [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] Game 1 victory in [[2019 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-06-06 |title=Brutal reality behind Origin star's post-game tears |work=[[News.com.au]] |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/brutal-reality-behind-origin-stars-postgame-tears/news-story/7cc7eebf350433a1ee2515c1fee3443e |url-status=live |access-date=2019-06-17 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190617063821/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/brutal-reality-behind-origin-stars-postgame-tears/news-story/7cc7eebf350433a1ee2515c1fee3443e |archive-date=2019-06-17}}</ref>

Mbye collapsed at a hotel in Perth before the second game of the [[2019 State of Origin series]].  According to reports, Mbye was found slumped on the floor in the Queensland team's hotel. Mbye was lucky to have been found so quickly after falling into an anaphylactic shock, assumed to be the result of an allergic reaction.  Mbye was later medically cleared to play in Game 2 of the series as Queensland lost 38-6 at the new [[Perth Stadium]].  Mbye was retained for Game 3 of the series as Queensland lost the match and the series overall in the final seconds of the game.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Pengilly |first=Adam |date=2019-06-23 |title=Trbojevic and Addo-Carr lead origin try spree |work=[[Sydney Morning Herald]] |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/trbojevic-and-addo-carr-lead-try-spree-as-blues-level-series-in-perth-20190623-p520gn.html/ |url-status=live |url-access=limited |access-date=2019-07-24 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190626114617/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/trbojevic-and-addo-carr-lead-try-spree-as-blues-level-series-in-perth-20190623-p520gn.html |archive-date=2019-06-26}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Johnson |first=Paul |date=2019-07-10 |title=Emotional Blues legend Wayne Pearce opens up on his son Mitchell's battle |work=[[news.com.au]] |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/origin/emotional-blues-legend-wayne-pearce-opens-up-on-his-son-mitchells-battle/news-story/0bdd173d0bf51730d60783a379922735/ |url-status=live |access-date=2019-07-24 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190713195743/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/origin/emotional-blues-legend-wayne-pearce-opens-up-on-his-son-mitchells-battle/news-story/0bdd173d0bf51730d60783a379922735 |archive-date=2019-07-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Moses Mbye nearly dies in horror incident - but still set to play for Queensland |work=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/state-of-origin-moses-mbye-nearly-dies-in-horror-incident-but-still-set-to-play-for-queensland/OISXE45H7UZ7EPXMTOS7RZ77YQ/ |url-status=live |access-date=2021-09-12 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210912071451/https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/state-of-origin-moses-mbye-nearly-dies-in-horror-incident-but-still-set-to-play-for-queensland/OISXE45H7UZ7EPXMTOS7RZ77YQ/ |archive-date=2021-09-12 |via=[[news.com.au]]}}</ref>

===2020===
Mbye made a total of 15 appearances for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2020 NRL season]] which saw the club finish 11th on the table.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Elbra |first=Tim |date=2020-09-30 |title=Wests Tigers doomed to another finals miss in 2021, Peter Sterling predicts |url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/wests-tigers-season-review-peter-sterling-luke-brooks-moses-mbye-james-tamou-michael-maguire/0ee11d5b-9955-45a8-aa24-b30a25fd3d49/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210128035031/https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/wests-tigers-season-review-peter-sterling-luke-brooks-moses-mbye-james-tamou-michael-maguire/0ee11d5b-9955-45a8-aa24-b30a25fd3d49 |archive-date=2021-01-28 |access-date=2021-10-06 |website=[[Nine's Wide World of Sports]]}}</ref>

===2021===
On 18 August, Mbye signed a contract to join [[St. George Illawarra]] starting in the 2022 season.  Mbye played a total of 22 matches for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished 13th and missed the finals.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Conrad |first=Alex |date=2021-10-06 |title=Wests Tigers fans savage the club's new-look logo |work=[[News.com.au]] |agency=NCA Newswire |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-fans-savage-the-clubs-newlook-logo/news-story/83f356c5f7c0d336d950d750f4e054b5/ |url-status=live |access-date=2021-10-06 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211006195525/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-fans-savage-the-clubs-newlook-logo/news-story/83f356c5f7c0d336d950d750f4e054b5 |archive-date=2021-10-06}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-18 |title='I was becoming a hindrance': Moses Mbye's candid confession after $800k Tigers exit |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/nrl-2021-news-moses-mbye-joins-st-george-illawarra-dragons-wests-tigers-exit-contract-news-transfer-centre/news-story/1c92a668f991389499d9a2c79d24e519/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211007023048/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/nrl-2021-news-moses-mbye-joins-st-george-illawarra-dragons-wests-tigers-exit-contract-news-transfer-centre/news-story/1c92a668f991389499d9a2c79d24e519 |archive-date=2021-10-07 |access-date=2021-10-06 |website=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 1 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Mbye made his club debut for [[St. George Illawarra]] in their 28-16 victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]] scoring a try.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McDonald |first=Darcie |date=2022-03-12 |title=Dragons' ugly side exposed before 'machine' stuns; Johnson's 'magic' not enough: 3 Big Hits |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-warriors-vs-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-updates-score-video-shaun-johnson-ben-hunt/news-story/be11a953f6f7a2a4e423c4f6c94cefb4/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220411125533/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-warriors-vs-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-updates-score-video-shaun-johnson-ben-hunt/news-story/be11a953f6f7a2a4e423c4f6c94cefb4 |archive-date=2022-04-11 |access-date=2022-04-11 |website=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]}}</ref>
Mbye played 22 games for the club in 2022 as they finished 10th on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-st-george-illawarra-dragons-season-review/l821xem9o1l8u8ylavtd3ono/|title=NRL 2022: St George Illawarra Dragons season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=13 September 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
On 24 July, it was announced that Mbye had signed a two-and-half-year deal to join English side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] with immediate effect. Mbye made his club debut for St Helens in round 20 of the 2023 Super League season. St Helens would defeat [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] 22-18.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23674101.saints-confirm-signing-moses-mbye-replace-joey-lussick/|title=Saints confirm signing of Moses Mbye to replace Joey Lussick|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=23 July 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
In round 21 of the 2024 Super League season, Mbye kicked the winning drop goal for St Helens in their 17-16 golden point extra-time victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2024/08/09/moses-mbyes-drop-goal-edges-st-helens-to-narrow-win-over-salford/|title=Moses Mbye's drop-goal edges St Helens to narrow win over Salford|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=9 August 2024 }}</ref>
Mbye played 23 games for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. He played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
'Soul destroying': Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2025===
On 28 August, Mbye announced he would be leaving St Helens at the end of the 2025 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2025/08/28/moses-mbye-to-leave-saints-at-the-end-of-2025/|title=St.Helens R.F.C. can confirm that Moses Mbye will be leaving the Club at the end of the 2025 Betfred Super League season|website=www.saintsrlfc.com}}</ref>
Mbye played 25 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs 20-12 semi-final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
